title: Roomio Photo-Texture Mapping contract (Agent H)
description: Contract for turning a user's furniture photo into a tiling PBR material and applying it to a Roomio archetype's material slot. PUBLISHED API: Agent H will not change its shape without announcing in roomio.txt and waiting for acks from A (FurnitureItem owner), C (persistence), and G (rendering). Three decoupled stages: T1 detect+crop (REUSES Agent B's detection; H does NOT rebuild object detection), T2 texture-ify (seamless tile + de-light + derive albedo/roughness/normal), T3 apply to a material slot with world-space tiling. IMAGE BYTES ARE NEVER EMBEDDED IN A DESIGN: the design stores REFERENCES (content-hash asset ids) and the bytes live in a content-addressed asset store (local-first, keyed `roomio.asset.<sha256>`, behind Agent C's StorageAdapter shape).

AssetRef.description: A reference to image bytes stored OUT-OF-BAND in the content-addressed asset store (key = `roomio.asset.<sha256>`). NEVER the bytes themselves. Content-hash so identical photos dedupe.

ColorSpace.description: Texture color space. albedo/base-color maps are 'srgb'; data maps (roughness/normal/metalness) are 'linear'.

Slot.description: Which material ROLE on the target archetype receives the texture. Roomio's parametric builders (src/three/Furniture3D.tsx) compose primitive meshes with NO named slots today; H defines this taxonomy. 'body' = the PRIMARY surface that uses the raw item color (sofa fabric body, table/desk top, cabinet body, chair seat, bed duvet). Apply to 'body' by default; other roles are opt-in once A tags meshes (see roomio.txt REQUEST -> AGENT-A).

PbrMaps.description: The three derived PBR maps. albedo is sRGB (de-lit base color); roughness + normal are LINEAR data maps. metalness optional (omit for dielectric fabric/wood). Each value is an AssetRef — never inline bytes.

Tiling.description: World-space tiling controls. repeat_cm = how many real-world centimeters ONE tile spans (mirrors the floor's areaCm contract in src/three/textures.ts: at apply time `texture.repeat` is derived per-mesh as worldDimCm / repeat_cm, so pattern density is physically consistent across differently-sized meshes). rotation_deg rotates the texture about its center. These are the two user-facing scale controls the brief requires.

TextureJob.description: T1 input: a detect+crop job. source_photo + bbox come from Agent B's detection result (shared/detection_schema.json). H crops CLIENT-SIDE from bbox (pixel [x,y,w,h] in the result's image.width/height downscaled space) + the user's original photo. No edit to B's pipeline.

source_photo.description: AssetRef to the original uploaded photo (optional; may be transient).

bbox.description: From Agent B: pixel [x, y, w, h] in the detection result's image.width/height (downscaled) space.

target_archetype_id.description: A real catalog id from shared/archetypes.json (e.g. 'sofa-3', 'table-coffee'). NOT the brief's placeholder 'sofa_3_seater'.

AppliedTexture.description: The PERSISTED slice: this is the ADDITIVE optional `texture` field on Agent A's FurnitureItem (src/types.ts). Round-trips verbatim through Agent C's persistence (coerce passes `interior` through; updateFurniture spread-merges; migrate only allowlists TOP-LEVEL envelope keys). Small by construction — references only, no bytes.

asset_id.description: Content hash of the source crop — stable identity for dedup + persistence.

status.description: Pipeline/UV quality status. ok = applied cleanly; needs_uv = had to fall back to triplanar; low_quality = crop was poor (folds/shadows).

description_examples: A texture_job describes the T1 input; an applied_texture is what lands (additively) on a FurnitureItem and persists.
